name: ◁ Samara’s No-Bake Chocolate Biscuit Cake
servings: prep time: 20 minutes, plus 4 hours chilling

ingredients.item: ½ cup (50 g) unsweetened cocoa powder
ingredients.original: ½ cup (50 g) unsweetened cocoa powder

ingredients.item: 12½ ounces (350 g) rectangular tea biscuits (see Notes)
ingredients.original: 12½ ounces (350 g) rectangular tea biscuits (see Notes)

ingredients.item:
FOR GARNISHING
½ cup (60 g) crushed pistachios
¼ cup (35 g) shaved or chopped chocolate of choice
ingredients.original:
FOR GARNISHING
½ cup (60 g) crushed pistachios
¼ cup (35 g) shaved or chopped chocolate of choice

ingredients.item: 1 In a medium saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the sugar and cocoa powder and whisk until combined, 4 to 5 minutes. Add 1 cup (240 ml) of the milk and continue to whisk for 5 minutes, or until the sauce starts to thicken and bubble. Remove the pan from the heat and whisk in the instant coffee until it is dissolved.
ingredients.original: 1 In a medium saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the sugar and cocoa powder and whisk until combined, 4 to 5 minutes. Add 1 cup (240 ml) of the milk and continue to whisk for 5 minutes, or until the sauce starts to thicken and bubble. Remove the pan from the heat and whisk in the instant coffee until it is dissolved.

ingredients.item: 2 Line the bottom and sides of a loaf pan (see Notes) with plastic wrap, ensuring there is at least 2 inches (5 cm) of plastic wrap overhanging the sides of the pan (to act as “handles” when releasing the pan before serving).
ingredients.original: 2 Line the bottom and sides of a loaf pan (see Notes) with plastic wrap, ensuring there is at least 2 inches (5 cm) of plastic wrap overhanging the sides of the pan (to act as “handles” when releasing the pan before serving).

ingredients.item: 3 Pour the remaining ½ cup (120 ml) milk into a small bowl. Start assembling the cake by dipping the tea biscuits in the milk for 1 second and arranging them on the bottom of the pan, side by side. Cover the biscuits with a layer of the chocolate sauce, ensuring all the biscuits are covered, then add another layer of biscuits dipped in milk, followed by another layer of chocolate sauce. Continue to layer the biscuits and chocolate sauce until the sauce is used up, with the top layer being chocolate sauce. You should have 5 or 6 biscuit layers if using a loaf pan.
ingredients.original: 3 Pour the remaining ½ cup (120 ml) milk into a small bowl. Start assembling the cake by dipping the tea biscuits in the milk for 1 second and arranging them on the bottom of the pan, side by side. Cover the biscuits with a layer of the chocolate sauce, ensuring all the biscuits are covered, then add another layer of biscuits dipped in milk, followed by another layer of chocolate sauce. Continue to layer the biscuits and chocolate sauce until the sauce is used up, with the top layer being chocolate sauce. You should have 5 or 6 biscuit layers if using a loaf pan.

ingredients.item: 4 Smooth the final layer of chocolate sauce using a butter knife. Cover the loaf pan with a separate piece of plastic wrap (not the overhanging plastic wrap) and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or overnight.
ingredients.original: 4 Smooth the final layer of chocolate sauce using a butter knife. Cover the loaf pan with a separate piece of plastic wrap (not the overhanging plastic wrap) and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or overnight.

ingredients.item: 5 Remove the top plastic wrap layer, and carefully use the overhanging plastic wrap to lift the cake out of the pan and onto a serving platter. The cake should be set enough to handle.
ingredients.original: 5 Remove the top plastic wrap layer, and carefully use the overhanging plastic wrap to lift the cake out of the pan and onto a serving platter. The cake should be set enough to handle.

ingredients.item: 6 Garnish with the crushed pistachios and shaved chocolate, then slice and serve.
ingredients.original: 6 Garnish with the crushed pistachios and shaved chocolate, then slice and serve.
